reason: pro63 claims some abortions are justified, challenging pro7's premise that only health exceptions permit abortion after viability
pro63b argues abortion can be justified on broader grounds, undermining pro7's restriction to health exceptions
pro27 asserts the Constitution does not define a fetus as a person, challenging the legal basis of pro7's protection claim
Claiming some abortions are justified contradicts the stance to support legislation ending all abortions, which assumes none are justified.
Stating that a fetus is not a person attacks the premise that abortion can be compared to killing an adult human.
Citing historical figures who opposed abortion contradicts the conclusion that abortion would be justified under the same reasoning as killing an adult.
Advocating legislation to end abortion opposes the claim that abortion could be justified by the same standards applied to killing an adult.
